title: elaphoglossum pseudovillosum
intro: Elaphoglossum pseudovillosum is a species of epiphytic fern in the family Dryopteridaceae, commonly known as the false-villow fern. This plant has been found to possess medicinal properties, with its rhizomes and roots used to treat various health issues. The rhizomes and roots of Elaphoglossum pseudovillosum are used to treat fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal problems, and are also believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, offering several health benefits. In traditional herbal preparations, the rhizomes and roots are often dried, crushed, and used to make teas, infusions, or added to soups to harness its medicinal properties.
